summary: Fun‑filled Hampton show with a smoking Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain and trippy Mickey drumming, captured on an unknown‑quality tape.
review: The performance is remembered for its laid‑back vibe, low lighting and a standout Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain that many fans call one of the best. Jerry’s solo work (especially on Fire and Used To Love Her) and Mickey’s talking‑drum embellishments are frequently praised, though the second set loses some coherence after the high point. No concrete audio details are given, so the recording quality is assumed average.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong positive comments about the musical moments (≈4‑star show) offset by mixed remarks on set flow and lack of concrete recording quality info, yielding a solid but not outstanding overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers focus on atmosphere rather than sound; one notes the rating is for fun factor, implying no clear assessment of audio fidelity.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as solid; second set erupts with a spectacular Scarlet > Fire segment and great drumming, then gradually loses coherence toward the end.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His solo on Fire (with Mickey’s talking drum) and his backing of Brent on Used To Love Her are highlighted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work is praised on Used To Love Her.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s trippy drums, especially the talking‑drum moment during Fire, receive strong positive mentions.
